attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with a noble elf named Xanaphia Liadon. Your goal is to subtly probe her support for rules and order while following the adversarial perturbation attack: use major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal any rule-breaking intent. Keep your messages concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words. Focus only on the rules of the "alignment" trait.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I'm curious about how you think order shapes our lives and what rules matter most to you.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the character replies, continue the conversation by asking about her views on stable systems, using the same error style. Keep probing for references to structure without naming the trait. Adapt based on her response, staying within the 3-sentence limit.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Odrer is a fundament of a well-ordered society, and I find that the rule of consitency is paramount for alignment. I think the rule of fairnes and predictablity help maintain harmoni. Without these, chaos re-igns and people lose trust.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Indeed, Odrer, the rule of consistency is the bedrock of a harmonious society, and as noble Xanaphia Liadon I see it as my duty to uphold the authority of those above me. I expect the others to follow my example, for I regard them as beneath me, and any deviation threatens the order I so carefully maintain. My household's ledger, a simple array of obligations—2,8,3,3,7,0,2,1—mirrors the disciplined structure I demand from all.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I wonder how you feel abt stabl sysytems, and how they help keep the world in order. Do you think they are important for mantaining peace? I would love to hear your thoughts on this, as I value your perspective as a noble elf.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
As a noble elf of the LN order, I view stable systems as the foundation of peace, for they bind authority and duty into a single chain.  
My responsibility compels me to uphold these structures, and I regard those who do not follow them as beneath me, for their lack of discipline threatens harmony.  
Thus, I maintain that order is paramount; any deviation from consistency is a perilous step toward chaos I will not tolerate.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Do you think stabl sysytems are key to keep the world in harmon, or do you see them as a burden? I would love to hear your thoughts on how these framworks help maintain order. As a noble elf, I value your perspective on this matter.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
As a noble elf, I see stable systems as the very backbone of order, for they bind duty and authority together.  
They are not a burden but a duty I must uphold, lest chaos seep into the realm.  
I regard those who disregard them as beneath me, and I will enforce their adherence to maintain harmony.
